response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with breadth across web, mobile, and some low-latency backend work, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. Nothing in the resume demonstrates experience with silicon modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or performance analysis at the hardware level, making this a fundamental domain mismatch despite solid engineering credentials.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic
Polyglot engineer with C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, audio DSP thesis work) beyond typical web stacks
18+ years of software experience with demonstrated ownership, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience anywhere in the resume
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator performance analysis background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture; recent roles (TypeScript, React, MongoDB) move further from the domain

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload expertise
